text: Judah left his brothers and settled near an Adullamite man named Hirah.
There Judah met and married a Canaanite woman, daughter of Shua.
She conceived and bore a son named Er.
She conceived again and bore a son named Onan.
She bore a third son named Shelah, at Kezib.
Judah got a wife for Er, his firstborn, named Tamar.
Er was wicked in the LORD's sight, and the LORD put him to death.
Judah told Onan to marry Tamar and raise up offspring for his dead brother.
Knowing the child would not be his, Onan let his seed go to waste rather than give his brother offspring.
What he did was wicked in the LORD's sight, and He put Onan to death also.
Judah told Tamar to live as a widow in her father's house until Shelah grew up, fearing he too might die; but he never intended to give Shelah to her.
After a long time Judah's wife died; once comforted, he went with Hirah to his sheepshearers at Timnah.
Tamar was told her father-in-law was going up to Timnah to shear sheep.
She took off her widow's clothes, covered herself with a veil, and sat at the entrance to Enaim, seeing Shelah had not been given to her.
Judah saw her and thought she was a prostitute, since her face was covered.
He went to her and offered to sleep with her, not knowing she was his daughter-in-law; she asked what he would pay.
He offered a young goat from his flock; she asked for a pledge until he sent it.
She asked for his seal, cord, and staff as pledge; he gave them and slept with her, and she conceived.
She went away, removed her veil, and put her widow's clothes back on.
Judah sent the goat by his friend Hirah to get back his pledge, but Hirah could not find her.
Hirah asked locals about the shrine prostitute at Enaim; they said none had been there.
Hirah returned and reported he could not find her, and the men said there was no such woman there.
Judah said, let her keep the items then, lest we be a laughingstock; I sent the goat, but you couldn't find her.
About three months later Judah was told Tamar was pregnant by prostitution; Judah ordered her burned.
As she was brought out, she sent word to her father-in-law that the owner of these items made her pregnant, asking him to identify the seal, cord, and staff.
Judah recognized them and said she was more righteous than he, since he had not given her Shelah; he did not sleep with her again.
When it was time to give birth, there were twins in her womb.
During labor one baby put out a hand, and the midwife tied a scarlet thread on it, saying this one came out first.
But he drew back his hand, and his brother came out first; she said, so this is how you have broken out, and he was named Perez.
Then his brother with the scarlet thread came out, and he was named Zerah.
